records.ety: [OE. bæe, beere, AS. bǣr, bǣre; akin to D. baar, OHG. bāra, G. bahre, Icel barar, Dutch baare, L. feretrum, Gr. <?/, from the same <?/<?/ bear to produce. See 1st Bear, and cf. Barrow.]

defs: A handbarrow or portable frame on which a corpse is placed or borne to the grave.
A count of forty threads in the warp or chain of woolen cloth.
